feedback.details: CRITICAL BUG: The `preview` field is completely absent from the GET /api/conversations response. Root cause: `backend/db/repository.py` `list_conversations()` function (line 186-189) uses `{k: r[k] for k in r.keys()}` to serialize aiosqlite.Row objects, but aiosqlite.Row.keys() does NOT include computed/aliased columns from SQL subqueries. The SQL query correctly aliases the subquery as `preview`, and direct SQLite verification confirms the data IS present in the DB (e.g., 'Quick test' conversation has preview 'Hello! It looks like you're just testing things out...'). However, every API response for /api/conversations returns only ['id', 'title', 'created_at', 'updated_at'] — preview is never present. As a result, the sidebar shows zero preview strings for any conversation. Title and relative timestamp render correctly, and empty-state shows 'No conversations yet', but the required preview string criterion is completely unmet.

Correctly implements 260px sidebar (flex-shrink: 0) and flex-fill main area. Root background #0a0a0f confirmed in CSS. Sidebar background #111827 confirmed. overflow: hidden prevents horizontal overflow. Mobile responsive: at max-width 767px sidebar becomes position:fixed with translateX(-260px) and toggles with .open class. Hamburger button shown on mobile. No overflow issues at 375px viewport.

React Router v6 correctly configured in App.tsx with '/' route rendering LandingPage (AppLayout with no conversationId) and '/c/:conversationId' rendering ConversationPage. The '/' route renders the EmptyState component with a heading, description, and 3 sample prompt cards — not a blank white screen. No redirect to /c/:id on '/' which is acceptable per the spec. ChatInput is hidden when no conversationId is set.

useMessages hook fetches via getConversation(id) which hits GET /api/conversations/{id}. For valid IDs, the API returns conversation + messages array correctly. Verified 'What is retrieval-augmented generation?' returns 2 messages. For non-existent IDs, API returns HTTP 404 with detail 'Conversation not found'; the frontend catches this error and renders ErrorState with the error message and a Retry button. The error message displayed will be 'API error 404: {"detail":"Conversation not found"}' which is visible but verbose.

During fetch (loading=true), Sidebar renders 4 SkeletonRow components. Each row contains 3 .skeleton divs (14px height title, 11px timestamp, 11px preview rows). The .skeleton class uses the shimmer animation (CSS @keyframes shimmer with background-position animation). 4 rows exceeds the 3-row minimum. No spinner used.

ConvItem renders with borderLeft: isActive ? '3px solid #3b82f6' : '3px solid transparent' — exactly 3px blue border. Active background is #1e293b vs transparent for inactive. Hover shows rgba(30,41,59,0.6). Switching conversations updates activeConversationId prop which triggers immediate re-render of all items.

Delete button appears on hover (hovered state via onMouseEnter/Leave). Clicking calls onDeleteRequest which sets confirmId and shows ConfirmDialog modal. Dialog has Cancel and Delete buttons. On confirm, calls deleteConversation() (DELETE /api/conversations/{id}), then calls refetch() to update list without reload. If response is non-2xx, setDeleteError(true) shows 'Failed to delete. Please try again.' in the dialog. DELETE API returns 204 on success and 404 for non-existent IDs. One edge case: the check `if (!res.ok && res.status !== 204)` is slightly redundant (204 is a success status), but functionally correct.

Blue (#3b82f6) full-width button pinned to sidebar top. On click, calls createConversation() (POST /api/conversations), then refetch() to update list, then navigate(`/c/${conv.id}`). On POST failure, shows 'Could not create conversation. Please try again.' in red text below the button. Route does not change on error. Verified via API: POST returns 201 with new conversation object.

User messages: justifyContent flex-end, background #2563eb, color #f1f5f9 (white), maxWidth 70%. Assistant messages: justifyContent flex-start, background #1e293b, color #f1f5f9, maxWidth 80%. Border-radius uses 18px values (not Tailwind rounded-2xl of 16px, but close). Asymmetric corners (18/18/4/18 for user, 18/18/18/4 for assistant) create a chat-bubble aesthetic. Word-break: break-word applied.

SkeletonMessages renders 4 rows alternating flex-end and flex-start alignment, with varying widths mimicking real message bubbles. Each row contains multiple .skeleton divs. CSS shimmer animation applied. maxWidth matches actual message widths (70% for user-side, 80% for assistant-side). Loading state (showSkeleton = loading && !showEmpty) correctly gates display.

MarkdownRenderer uses ReactMarkdown with remarkGfm plugin. Custom components override the code renderer for syntax highlighting. CSS in globals.css styles .assistant-content with p, ul, ol, li, h1-h3, strong, em, blockquote selectors. Bold/italic/lists/code all handled. The remark-gfm plugin enables GFM-specific features like tables and strikethrough.

CodeBlock component wraps SyntaxHighlighter (Prism) with oneDark theme, customStyle background #0d1117. Header bar shows language label (.code-lang-label) and CopyButton. For language-tagged fences, SyntaxHighlighter renders with full highlighting. For unlabeled fences (match=null), CodeBlock renders a plain <pre> with same #0d1117 background and shows 'plaintext' as language label. Copy-to-clipboard works with clipboard API + execCommand fallback.

autoScrollRef tracks whether auto-scroll is enabled. handleScroll calculates distFromBottom = scrollHeight - scrollTop - clientHeight and sets autoScrollRef.current = distFromBottom < 100. useEffect triggers scrollToBottom() when messages.length or streamingContent changes, only if autoScrollRef.current is true. When user sends a message, autoScrollRef.current is reset to true. One minor issue: after loading completes, scrollToBottom('instant') is called with a setTimeout(50) — this could cause a brief flash.

adjustHeight sets el.style.height = 'auto' then Math.min(el.scrollHeight, 144). 144px = 6 lines × 24px lineHeight. overflowY switches to 'auto' when scrollHeight > maxH. After send, height reset to 'auto' and overflowY to 'hidden'. rows={1} for initial single-line height.

The component uses React state `focused` (set true on onFocus, false on onBlur) to apply box-shadow: '0 0 0 2px #3b82f6' via inline style to both the container div and the textarea element itself. The CSS class `.chat-input-wrapper:focus-within` exists in globals.css but the container div has no className assigned — this CSS rule is dead code. However, the inline-style approach achieves the same visual result and the criteria is met functionally. Glow absent when not focused or when disabled.

An absolute-positioned div with height:120, linear-gradient(to bottom, transparent, #0a0a0f), pointerEvents:none, zIndex:1 sits above the message list. The chat input has zIndex:2 (above gradient). Message list has paddingBottom:140 so the last message scrolls past the gradient when at bottom. The gradient color #0a0a0f matches the root background. Gradient is visible during scroll and doesn't permanently obscure the last message.

handleKeyDown: `if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) { e.preventDefault(); handleSend() }`. Shift+Enter falls through to default textarea behavior (newline). handleSend(): `const content = el.value.trim(); if (!content || isDisabled) return` — empty/whitespace strings are rejected before calling onSend, preventing API calls on empty input.

isDisabled = disabled || isStreaming. Both textarea and button have `disabled={isDisabled}` prop. Container opacity drops to 0.7. Button background changes to #1e293b. Textarea cursor: not-allowed. handleSend() also checks `isDisabled` as a guard, preventing any API call even if events fire. The disabled attribute on the textarea prevents keyboard input and keyboard events in standard browsers.

Google Fonts Inter (weights 400/500/600/700) loaded via <link> in index.html. CSS font-family: 'Inter', -apple-system, ... applied to html/body/#root. --text-primary: #f1f5f9 applied as color to body. --text-secondary: #94a3b8 used for timestamps (conv items), code labels, placeholder text. Color variables confirmed in compiled CSS output.

overallSummary: The implementation is high quality across almost all criteria — the routing, layout, skeleton loaders, delete confirmation, markdown rendering, syntax highlighting, chat input, and streaming are all well-implemented. The sprint fails due to a single backend bug: the `list_conversations` function in `repository.py` serializes aiosqlite.Row using `r.keys()`, which does not include subquery alias columns. The `preview` subquery alias is silently dropped, causing the API to return conversations without a `preview` field. As a result, the sidebar never displays preview text for any conversation, failing the `sidebar_conversation_list_populated` criterion (scored 4/10). Fix: in `list_conversations`, explicitly include the preview column via `dict(r)` instead of the `{k: r[k] for k in r.keys()}` pattern, or use `cursor.description` to get all column names including aliases.
